MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-22-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302,PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO ENFORCE DEFENDANTS' CCP SECTION 998 OFFER TO COMPROMISE IS GRANTED BECAUSE THE RECORD INDICATES THAT WHEN PLAINTIFF ACCEPTED THE OFFER, HER ACCEPTANCE WAS UNEQUIVOCAL AND UNCONDITIONAL. (DECL. RUBENSTEIN 10, EX. A; FEY DECL. 5.) DEFENDANTS' RELIANCE UPON BIAS V. WRIGHT IS UNPERSUASIVE BECAUSE IN THAT CASE THE OFFEREE'S DECLARATION REVEALED THAT HER ACCEPTANCE WAS CONDITIONED ON A TERM THAT WAS NOT INCLUDED IN THE CCP SECTION 998 OFFER. (BIAS V. WRIGHT 103 (2002) 103 CAL.APP.4TH 811, 820.) THE PARTIES' FAILURE TO REACH A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF'S ACCEPTANCE OF THE CCP SECTION 998 OFFER WAS EQUIVOCAL OR UNCERTAIN. ADDITIONALLY, THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANTS, AT ANY POINT, REVOKED THE OFFER. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489. (302/EG)
ORDER RE: MOTION TO ENFORCE CCP SECTION 998 OFFER TO COMPROMISE

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Ccp 998 Offer
Off Calendar Dec-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Stella Epling of North Beach Law telephone number 415-433-1448 for Plaintiff Susan Rubenstein.
David Hamerslough of Rossi Hamerslough Reischl and Chuck telephone number 408-261-4252 for Defendant.

The parties reported that they have a settlement agreement. Mr. Hamerslough noted that 998 calls for settlement, but not for judgment to be entered. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SUSAN RUBENSTEIN'S Motion To Enforce CCP Section 998 Offer to Compromise is granted. Plaintiff's motion to enforce Defendants' CCP section 998 offer to compromise is granted because the record indicates that when Plaintiff accepted the offer, her acceptance was unequivocal and unconditional. (Decl. Rubenstein 10, Ex. A; Fey Decl. 5.) Defendants' reliance upon Bias v. Wright is unpersuasive because in that case the offeree's declaration revealed that her acceptance was conditioned on a term that was not included in the CCP section 998 offer. (Bias v. Wright 103 (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 811, 820.) The parties' failure to reach a settlement agreement does not demonstrate that Plaintiff's acceptance of the CCP section 998 offer was equivocal or uncertain. Additionally, there is no evidence that Defendants, at any point, revoked the offer. The prevailing party is provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)
